ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was collected */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network device from which the metrics were collected */,
  "network_segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network segment from which the metrics were collected */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Network protocol for which the metrics are collected */,
  "service" TEXT /* Service for which the metrics are collected */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the network segment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the metric (e.g., normal, warning, critical) */,
  "anomaly_detected" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if an anomaly was detected */,
  "accept" REAL /* Latency for accept operation */,
  "bind" REAL /* Latency for bind operation */,
  "clone" REAL /* Latency for clone operation */,
  "close" REAL /* Latency for close operation */,
  "connect" REAL /* Latency for connect operation */,
  "dup" REAL /* Latency for duplicate operation */,
  "exit" REAL /* Latency for exit operation */,
  "fcntl" REAL /* Latency for fcntl operation */,
  "futex" REAL /* Latency for futex operation */,
  "getpid" REAL /* Process ID of the request */,
  "getsockname" REAL /* Socket name */,
  "getsockopt" REAL /* Socket option value */,
  "ioctl" REAL /* IO control data */,
  "list" REAL /* List of operations */,
  "madvise" REAL /* Memory advice data */,
  "mprotect" REAL /* Memory protection data */,
  "newfstat" REAL /* New file status data */,
  "newstat" REAL /* New stat data */,
  "open" REAL /* Open operation latency */,
  "poll" REAL /* Poll operation latency */,
  "read" REAL /* Read operation latency */,
  "recvfrom" REAL /* Receive from operation latency */,
  "recvmsg" REAL /* Receive message operation latency */,
  "select" REAL /* Select operation latency */,
  "sendto" REAL /* Send to operation latency */,
  "setsockopt" REAL /* Set socket option latency */,
  "shutdown" REAL /* Shutdown operation latency */,
  "socket" REAL /* Socket operation latency */,
  "write" REAL /* Write operation latency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., router, switch) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the device */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_segments" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network segment */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the network segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network segment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the network segment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the network segment (e.g., active, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" INTEGER /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON array of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who created the dashboard */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the dashboard was created */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* JSON array of user IDs to whom the dashboard is shared */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each metric entry
Timestamp of when the data was collected
ID of the network device from which the metrics were collected
ID of the network segment from which the metrics were collected
Network protocol for which the metrics are collected
Service for which the metrics are collected
Geographical location of the network segment
Status of the metric (e.g., normal, warning, critical)
Boolean flag to indicate if an anomaly was detected
Type of the device (e.g., router, switch)
Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive)

Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer, analyst)
JSON array of permissions associated with the role
User ID of the user who created the dashboard

JSON array of user IDs to whom the dashboard is shared
